INBX.headline: Large Drugmakers Eye Inhibrx Cancer Drugs Valued Over $8 Billion, Stock Surges
INBX.description: Inhibrx Biosciences is in early-stage discussions with multiple major pharmaceutical companies regarding a potential joint spin-off of its cancer drug INBRX-106 and another oncology asset, with combined valuations potentially exceeding $9 billion. INBRX-106 is being tested as a standalone therapy and in combination with Merck's Keytruda. The stock surged 39.86% to $116.76 on the news.

POET.headline: Stock Market Today, April 20: POET Technologies Jumps After Responding to Short Report and Clarifying PFIC Status
POET.description: POET Technologies stock surged 18.32% after the company issued a detailed rebuttal to a short-seller report and clarified PFIC tax concerns, addressing governance risks. However, investors remain focused on the company's early-stage commercialization, limited revenue, and ongoing funding needs as it develops photonic integrated circuits for AI data center connectivity.

NVTS.headline: Avis Brings Back The Short Squeeze — Navitas, Beyond Meat Join In
NVTS.description: Avis Budget Group (CAR) is leading a meme stock rally, surging 500% from lows in the $80s to $665, driven by short squeeze dynamics with near 100% short interest. Other heavily-shorted stocks including Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS), Beyond Meat (BYND), Lucid Group (LCID), Opendoor Technologies (OPEN), and ImmunityBio (IBRX) are also experiencing significant gains fueled by options gamma dynamics and retail speculation, despite weak fundamentals in many cases.

SMR.headline: I'm Calling It: NuScale Power Stock Is a Buy Before June 2
SMR.description: NuScale Power stock has declined 22% in 2026 despite a recent 40% surge. The author recommends buying before June 2, when management will present at a Royal Bank of Canada conference. Key catalysts include a May 7 earnings report and the June 2 presentation, where investors should monitor management's focus on long-term growth drivers like AI data center demand and the status of five exploratory NDAs with major tech companies.

SMR.headline: Should You Forget NuScale Power and Buy This Nuclear Stock Instead?
SMR.description: While NuScale Power is a leading small modular reactor (SMR) developer with a $4 billion market cap, competitor Oklo trades at a premium valuation of $11 billion, largely due to Sam Altman's involvement as chairman. Oklo's smaller reactor designs (15-50 MW) are better suited for AI data centers seeking direct power supply, whereas NuScale's larger designs (77 MW) target utility-scale deployments. Both companies represent promising SMR opportunities, but their different strategic approaches make

AXTI.headline: AXT Stock Stumbles After The Close: Here's Why
AXTI.description: AXT announced a public offering of common stock to support its subsidiary Beijing Tongmei Xtal Technology Co. and increase indium phosphide substrate production capacity. The stock declined 6.50% in after-hours trading to $73.51, despite being up over 6,500% in the past year and 381% year-to-date.

OKLO.headline: Should You Forget NuScale Power and Buy This Nuclear Stock Instead?
OKLO.description: While NuScale Power is a leading small modular reactor (SMR) developer with a $4 billion market cap, competitor Oklo trades at a premium valuation of $11 billion, largely due to Sam Altman's involvement as chairman. Oklo's smaller reactor designs (15-50 MW) are better suited for AI data centers seeking direct power supply, whereas NuScale's larger designs (77 MW) target utility-scale deployments. Both companies represent promising SMR opportunities, but their different strategic approaches make

GEV.headline: GE Vernova Stock Hits 52-Week High - Here's Why
GEV.description: GE Vernova (GEV) shares surged 7.74% to a 52-week high after beating Q1 2026 earnings expectations with adjusted EPS of $2.06 versus $1.88 estimate and revenue of $9.339 billion exceeding consensus. The company reported strong backlog growth of $13 billion sequentially to $163 billion, driven by surging demand in power and electrification segments. GE Vernova raised full-year 2026 guidance for revenue, EBITDA margin, and free cash flow, though the wind segment faced headwinds from tariffs and lo

GEV.headline: Stock Market Today: Dow Jones, S&P 500 Futures Rise After Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire—Tesla, Boeing, GE Vernova And AT&T In Focus (UPDATED)
GEV.description: U.S. stock futures rose on Wednesday following Tuesday's decline after President Trump extended the Iran ceasefire. Major indices showed gains with the Dow Jones up 0.56%, S&P 500 up 0.60%, and Nasdaq 100 up 0.76%. Key stocks in focus include Tesla, Boeing, and United Airlines, with analysts noting that crude oil prices will take time to normalize despite the ceasefire extension.

GEV.headline: What's Going On With GE Vernova On Tuesday?
GEV.description: GE Vernova (NYSE:GEV) is set to report earnings on April 22 with a Buy rating and $848.17 consensus price target. The company signed a wind-turbine supply agreement for a Spanish wind farm and recently received a price target increase to $1,080 from Susquehanna analyst Charles Minervino, citing strong nuclear and grid narratives. The stock is trading near its 52-week high with bullish technical indicators, though valuation appears stretched.

GEV.headline: Meta's $27 Billion Data Center Places the Spotlight on Natural Gas Stocks
GEV.description: Meta's $27 billion Louisiana data center investment is driving demand for natural gas and energy infrastructure. The company will fund seven new natural gas power plants and battery storage through a deal with Entergy. As AI workloads strain traditional grids, energy demand from data centers is projected to increase 50% by 2027 and 165% by 2030, benefiting natural gas and energy companies.

GEV.headline: Market Check: Industrials, Aerospace, and Infrastructure Earnings in Focus
GEV.description: Industrial, aerospace, and infrastructure companies are reporting earnings with mixed outlooks. 3M faces repeated disappointments with three consecutive quarters of misses. Boeing expects significant earnings decline of 29.5% despite sales growth. Lockheed Martin shows modest growth with focus on guidance. GE Vernova demonstrates strong momentum with 96.9% earnings surge driven by data center and energy infrastructure demand. Union Pacific expects stable growth with agriculture as a key driver.

VICR.headline: Vicor Corporation Reports Results for the First Quarter Ended March 31, 2026
VICR.description: Vicor Corporation reported Q1 2026 revenues of $113.0 million, up 20.2% year-over-year, with gross margins expanding to 55.2%. The company achieved net income of $20.7 million ($0.44 per diluted share) and saw backlog surge 70% sequentially to $301 million, driven by demand in high-performance computing and aerospace/defense sectors. The company is expanding manufacturing capacity with additional equipment and planning a second fab.

LUNR.headline: Why Did Intuitive Machines Stock Pop Today?
LUNR.description: Intuitive Machines stock surged 6.2% following the U.S. Air Force's announcement of a $338.8 billion fiscal 2027 budget request, which includes a 124% increase in U.S. Space Force spending. The boost is driven by significant increases in satellite communications (+60% to $6.7B) and missile defense (+70% to $6.8B) spending, areas where Intuitive Machines and its subsidiary Lanteris are positioned to benefit as major contractors.

LUNR.headline: The Launch Squeeze Is Real: 5 Stocks To Watch As Space Bottleneck Tightens
LUNR.description: A significant bottleneck in launch capacity is driving up prices across the space industry. SpaceX has raised Falcon 9 pricing, while competitors like Rocket Lab face delays. The shortage creates opportunities for launch providers and suppliers but pressures constellation operators. Key beneficiaries include Rocket Lab, Karman Holdings, and Firefly Aerospace, while AST SpaceMobile faces risks from Blue Origin's New Glenn failures.

ASTS.headline: ASTS Gains As FCC Approval Validates SpaceMobile Technology
ASTS.description: AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) shares surged 7.37% following FCC approval to deploy and operate up to 248 satellites for direct-to-device cellular broadband coverage in partnership with Verizon, AT&T, and FirstNet. The approval validates the company's technology, though a recent BlueBird 7 satellite was placed in a lower-than-planned orbit and will be de-orbited, with costs expected to be recovered through insurance.

ASTS.headline: AST SpaceMobile Drops 15% After Blue Origin Satellite Mishap
ASTS.description: AST SpaceMobile's stock plummeted 15% after Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket deployed the BlueBird 7 satellite into a lower-than-planned orbit on April 19, forcing de-orbiting of the satellite. Despite this setback and being behind schedule on its 2026 deployment targets, the company maintains strong fundamentals with $3.9 billion in liquidity, $1.2 billion in contracted revenue commitments, and strategic partnerships with major telecom providers.

ASTS.headline: AST SpaceMobile Stock Could Soar If This Happens Soon
ASTS.description: AST SpaceMobile faces critical timing challenges as the BlueBird 7 satellite delay raises questions about its rollout story. The company's stock, already priced for major success, now depends heavily on launch cadence and upcoming milestones to maintain investor confidence.

ASTS.headline: Stock Market Today, April 20: AST SpaceMobile Falls After BlueBird 7 Satellite Fails to Reach Planned Orbit
ASTS.description: AST SpaceMobile stock fell 5.30% on April 20, 2026, after its BlueBird 7 satellite failed to reach its planned orbit during a Blue Origin launch. The satellite will be destroyed in atmospheric re-entry, delaying the company's satellite deployment plans despite insurance coverage. The failure dampened investor sentiment despite the stock's 250% gain over the past 12 months.

ASTS.headline: Stock Market Today, April 20: Market Rebound Stumbles as Oil Disruption Continues
ASTS.description: U.S. stock markets pulled back on April 20, 2026, as U.S.-Iran tensions reignited concerns about Middle East stability. The S&P 500 fell 0.24%, Nasdaq dropped 0.26%, and the Dow edged down 0.01%. Oil prices surged nearly 6% to $88.85/barrel, boosting energy stocks while travel stocks declined. Key movers included American Airlines rejecting a United merger, Atlassian rallying after a year-long decline, and Apple falling in after-hours trading following Tim Cook's announced September departure.

ASTS.headline: The Launch Squeeze Is Real: 5 Stocks To Watch As Space Bottleneck Tightens
ASTS.description: A significant bottleneck in launch capacity is driving up prices across the space industry. SpaceX has raised Falcon 9 pricing, while competitors like Rocket Lab face delays. The shortage creates opportunities for launch providers and suppliers but pressures constellation operators. Key beneficiaries include Rocket Lab, Karman Holdings, and Firefly Aerospace, while AST SpaceMobile faces risks from Blue Origin's New Glenn failures.

QBTS.headline: Rigetti Computing vs. D-Wave Quantum: Navigating Volatile Revenue Trends
QBTS.description: Rigetti Computing and D-Wave Quantum, two early-stage quantum computing companies, show contrasting revenue trajectories. D-Wave generated $24 million in annual revenue compared to Rigetti's $7 million, driven by a significant Q1 2025 spike. Both companies are unprofitable and face volatile quarterly results, but the key question for investors is which can achieve consistent growth and scale fastest in this emerging technology sector.
